subject: SOC
course_notes: (Permission Required Prior to Enrollment) In this course, we will examine theories that address how status, power, and legitimacy processes operate in small group interaction and how these interactions on the micro level link to social inequality on the meso and macro levels. We will see the development and extension of theories in the status, power, and legitimacy areas, as well as review the empirical support for these theories. We will also discuss the various new directions in each of these areas. This course will: - Increase students' understanding of how sociological theories are developed, tested, and refined, and apply status, power, or legitimacy theories to a substantive area of their interest. - Deepen students' knowledge of sociological research methods through engaging with group processes studies that draw on a wide range of methodological approaches. - Improve students' written and oral communication skills through discussions and writing assignments.
